messages.content:
A user asks a generative AI model to create a picture of an ice cube in a hot frying pan. However, instead of showing the ice melting into water, the ice is still shown as a solid cube.

Why did this happen?

The user's prompt did not include a persona.

The model does not understand cause and effect.

The model has been trained on too much data.

The user's grammar was not entirely correct.

I don't know this yet.

It is likely that the generative AI model may not have the capability to accurately simulate the process of ice melting in a hot frying pan. The model may not have been specifically trained to understand the physical properties and reactions of ice when exposed to heat, leading to the unexpected result of showing the ice cube remaining solid in the frying pan. Additionally, the model may not have a contextual understanding of cause and effect relationships, further contributing to the inaccurate depiction of the scenario.

In addition to moving to the cloud, what is one thing a company must do to get real value from a Large Language Model?

Automate repetitive tasks.

Build out security plans.

Generate new algorithms.

Have high quality data.

I don't know this yet.

To get real value from a Large Language Model, one important thing a company must do is to have high quality data. Large Language Models rely on vast amounts of data to train and generate outputs, so having clean, relevant, and high-quality data is essential for maximizing the performance and efficacy of the model. Additionally, ensuring that the data is diverse and representative can help the model generate more accurate and meaningful insights.

An Accenture client wishes to use generative AI to enhance its business. The Client Account Lead reviews the client's goals and recommends a boosting approach.

What does boosting refer to in this case?

building the client a fully customized Gen AI model from the ground up

purchasing a large, general-purpose Gen AI model without customization

upgrading the client's PCs to handle more complex Gen AI models

customizing an existing Gen AI model using the client's own data

I don't know this yet.

In this case, boosting refers to customizing an existing Generative AI model using the client's own data. By fine-tuning or boosting an existing model with the client's specific data and requirements, the model can be optimized to better meet the client's unique business goals and needs. This approach allows for more tailored and effective use of Generative AI technology to enhance the client's business processes and outcomes.

What is an advantage of a large commercial generative AI model such as ChatGPT or Google Bard?

They are ready to use and have little to no cost.

They are free from any copyright concerns.

They are highly specialized to specific use cases.

They protect organizations' proprietary data.

I don't know this yet.

One advantage of a large commercial generative AI model like ChatGPT or Google Bard is that they are ready to use and have little to no cost associated with them. These models are pre-trained on vast amounts of data and readily available for use, saving organizations time and resources that would otherwise be spent on training a model from scratch. This accessibility can make it easier for businesses to leverage generative AI technology without significant upfront investment.

What does it mean to take a holistic approach to AI?

using a combination of generative, predictive, and diagnostic AI

using AI to perform all work actions without human intervention

building every client a fully customized AI model from the ground up

uploading company data into public chatbots to help train AI models

I don't know this yet.

To take a holistic approach to AI means to consider the broader context and interconnectedness of various aspects of artificial intelligence implementation. It involves looking at AI technologies not in isolation, but as part of a larger system or strategy that encompasses data, algorithms, models, processes, and overall business objectives. This approach considers the full lifecycle of AI implementation, including data collection, model training, deployment, monitoring, and continuous improvement. It also involves addressing ethical, legal, and societal implications of AI applications to ensure responsible and sustainable deployment. Taking a holistic approach to AI can help organizations maximize the benefits of AI technology while mitigating potential risks and challenges.

A digital manufacturing company wants to use artificial intelligence (AI) to improve the accuracy of their quality control functions and reduce the number of faulty products reaching the market.

Which form of AI should be used in this situation?

general AI

predictive AI

generative AI

diagnostic AI

I don't know this yet.

In this situation, predictive AI would be the most appropriate form of AI to use. Predictive AI involves using algorithms and data to make predictions about future outcomes based on historical data. In the context of quality control in manufacturing, predictive AI can analyze data from various sources such as production lines, sensor readings, and product inspections to identify patterns and trends that may indicate potential quality issues. By predicting which products are likely to be faulty, the company can take proactive measures to address quality issues before they reach the market, thereby improving accuracy and reducing the number of faulty products.

An employee is creating an email for a client using a chatbot. After receiving the first response, the employee wants to refine it and asks the chatbot, "Could you rewrite it to be more formal?"

What will happen next?

The chatbot will ask the employee to repeat the question and ask for an example before responding.

The chatbot will track the context and history of previous statements and make the result more formal.

The employee will also need to use a search engine to demonstrate the formal language they want.

The employee will need to reword the question with specific terminology for the chatbot.

I don't know this yet.

The chatbot, designed to assist with language refinement, will track the context and history of previous statements and then provide a response aimed at making the content more formal based on the initial message. This functionality allows the employee to easily refine the email content while maintaining professionalism and formality in communication with the client.

An employee is creating an email for a client using a chatbot. After receiving the first response, the employee wants to refine it and asks the chatbot, "Could you rewrite it to be more formal?"

What will happen next?

-The chatbot will ask the employee to repeat the question and ask for an example before responding.

-The chatbot will track the context and history of previous statements and make the result more formal.

-The employee will also need to use a search engine to demonstrate the formal language they want.

-The employee will need to reword the question with specific terminology for the chatbot.
The chatbot will track the context and history of previous statements and make the result more formal.

What is something responsible AI can help mitigate?

-repetitive statements

-security risk

-vague prompts

-unintended bias

Responsible AI can help mitigate unintended bias. By implementing ethical AI practices, organizations can work to identify and eliminate bias in AI algorithms and decision-making processes, ensuring fair and equitable outcomes for all users and stakeholders. By proactively addressing bias, organizations can promote transparency, accountability, and trust in AI systems.

A researcher is using a generative AI tool to find out about an event that happened two days ago.

What is likely to happen?

-There may be too many responses to sort through.

-The tool will produce one very specific summary.

-The tool may not know about anything so recent.

-There will be a few responses in a randomized order.

The tool may not know about anything so recent. Generative AI models are trained on data up to a certain point in time and may not have real-time information on events that occurred just two days ago. Therefore, it is possible that the tool may not be able to provide information about a recent event.

A researcher is using a generative AI tool to find out about an event that happened two days ago.

What is likely to happen?

-There may be too many responses to sort through.

-The tool will produce one very specific summary.

-The tool may not know about anything so recent.

-There will be a few responses in a randomized order.

The tool may not know about anything so recent. Generative AI models are typically trained on historical data up to a certain point in time and may not have real-time information on events that occurred just a few days ago. Therefore, it is likely that the tool may not have knowledge of such a recent event.

ChatGPT, Google Bard, and Anthropic Claude are examples of which type of generative AI model?

-extended reality model

-computer vision model

-large language model

-predictive model

ChatGPT, Google Bard, and Anthropic Claude are examples of large language models. These models are designed to generate human-like text based on the input they receive and are trained on vast amounts of text data to achieve this capability.

Why is it important to combine responsible AI with generative AI?

-Gen AI needs to store more data.

-Gen AI requires a new infrastructure.

-Gen AI tracks regulations more closely.

-Gen AI brings new areas of risk.

Combining responsible AI with generative AI is important because generative AI, by its very nature, introduces new areas of risk related to issues such as bias, ethical considerations, and misuse. Responsible AI practices help mitigate these risks by ensuring that AI technologies are developed and deployed in an ethical and accountable manner. By incorporating responsible AI principles, organizations can better address potential risks and challenges associated with generative AI, ultimately promoting the trustworthy and responsible use of AI technologies.

A large fashion retail chain wants to forecast consumer demand for products to optimize inventory and manage supply chains efficiently.

Which specific type of AI can meet the retailer's needs?

-generative AI

-predictive AI

-narrow AI

-reactive AI

Predictive AI can meet the retailer's needs in forecasting consumer demand for products. Predictive AI uses historical data to make predictions about future outcomes, which can be beneficial for forecasting consumer demand, optimizing inventory levels, and managing supply chains efficiently in the retail industry. By analyzing patterns and trends in data, predictive AI can help the fashion retail chain make informed decisions about product stocking, pricing, and promotions to meet consumer demand effectively.

An editor is using a generative AI application to summarize a document, however, the responses received from the application are not as expected.

What should the editor do in this case?

-Make the prompt as brief and vague as possible.

-Keep the prompt and continue to further refine it.

-Delete the prompt and create a new one from scratch.

-Ensure the prompt is free from grammatical errors.

In this case, the editor should keep the prompt and continue to further refine it. Generative AI models can sometimes require specific and detailed prompts to generate accurate and relevant responses. By refining and providing more context in the prompt, the editor may improve the quality and relevance of the responses received from the generative AI application. Deleting the prompt and creating a new one from scratch may not be necessary if the editor can iteratively refine the existing prompt to better align with the desired summary outcome.

Which term refers to applying structure and thought to interactions with generative AI, to steer it towards the results needed?

-cut-off point

-hallucination

-prompt engineering

-context length

The term that refers to applying structure and thought to interactions with generative AI, to steer it towards the results needed, is "prompt engineering." Prompt engineering involves crafting specific prompts or input cues that help guide the generative AI model to produce desired outputs or responses. By strategically designing prompts, users can influence the content, style, or focus of the AI-generated output to better align with their intended objectives. Prompt engineering plays a crucial role in effectively leveraging generative AI models for various tasks and applications.

A company wishes to use a large amount of its own proprietary information in a generative AI model.

Which type of system would be best suited for this client?

-deterministic system

-open system

-multi-party system

-closed system

For a company that wishes to use a large amount of its own proprietary information in a generative AI model, a closed system would be best suited for this client. A closed system refers to a computing environment that is isolated from external sources and has restrictions on data flow in and out of the system. In this context, the company's proprietary information can be securely stored and utilized within a closed system to safeguard the confidentiality and integrity of the data being used in the generative AI model. This helps protect sensitive information and intellectual property while allowing the company to leverage its proprietary data for AI applications.
